title: NOC 94153 – Photographic and film processors | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, the photographic and film processing sector in Canada is a specialized niche within the broader manufacturing and cultural industries. While digital photography has transformed the landscape, Photographic and Film Processors (NOC 94153) remain essential for high-end commercial photography, motion picture production, and specialized retail photofinishing. In 2026, the role has evolved to include sophisticated digital-to-analog transfers and the preservation of historical film archives. While classified as TEER 4, this occupation offers a stable career path for those who combine a keen eye for color with technical proficiency in chemical and digital imaging systems.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group941 – Processing and manufacturing machine operators

duties: Equipment Operation: Operating machinery to develop negatives, slides, and motion picture film, as well as printing black and white or color photographs.
Enlargement & Printing: Using photographic enlarging equipment to produce custom prints and enlargements from negatives.
Chemical Management: Measuring and mixing the precise chemicals required for various developing and fixing processes.
Quality Inspection: Inspecting finished prints or motion picture film to detect defects in developing, color accuracy, or sharpness.
Retouching & Correction: Retouching negatives or original prints to correct defects or enhance image quality.
2026 Update: Operating equipment to transfer traditional film to high-definition digital formats and using AI-assisted software for automated color correction.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Specialized Training: For employment in film processing laboratories, completion of a College Program in Photography or Film Processing is highly preferred in 2026.
On-the-Job Training: Retail photofinishing establishments generally provide several weeks of on-the-job training for machine tenders.
Technical Knowledge: Basic understanding of chemistry and strong color perception are essential for quality control.
salary_prospects: Note: Specialized technicians in motion picture laboratories in Vancouver or Toronto can earn upwards of $45,000 - $55,000 annually.
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) expects a "Balanced" market for specialized laboratory roles. While retail photography jobs have declined due to digital dominance, the revitalization of analog film among professionals and hobbyists, combined with high retirement rates in the industrial sector, is creating a steady stream of openings for technical processors through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is characterized by a "Limited" but stable demand, primarily driven by the film industry and high-end professional photography:
Ontario (Toronto): Limited prospects. Toronto is a major hub for film and television production, maintaining a steady need for specialized film laboratory technicians despite broader retail declines.
British Columbia (Vancouver): Moderate prospects. Driven by "Hollywood North," Vancouver's film processing labs are actively seeking technicians with experience in motion picture film development.
Quebec (Montreal): Stable demand. Supported by the province's vibrant arts, culture, and specialized professional scientific services.
Alberta: Moderate demand. Opportunities are concentrated in urban centers like Calgary and Edmonton, supporting professional and technical services.
Why Work in Canada as a Photographic and Film Processor?
Specialized Technical Skills: Gain expertise in both traditional chemical processing and 2026-grade digital imaging and retouching software.
Cultural Sector Integration: Work at the heart of Canada’s multi-billion dollar film and television industry.
High Stability in Labs: Professional labs offer consistent full-time hours (approximately 68% of workers are full-time) and higher wages than retail outlets.
Path to PR: Eligible for the 2026 TR to PR transition streams and specialized Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) streams for technical manufacturing workers.
pathways: TR to PR Pathway (April 2026): A newly launched 2026 initiative for 33,000 temporary residents. Photographic processors with 12 months of Canadian experience are eligible candidates under the "Essential Manufacturing and Technical" category.
Ontario Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): This NOC is frequently eligible for provincial nomination for those with a valid job offer in Ontario's professional and technical service sectors.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): Provides a direct route for processors working in British Columbia's thriving film and television industry.
Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A streamlined pathway for technical machine operators to obtain Permanent Residency with a provincial job offer.
